metrics.narrative: A detailed, jointly built ROI model exists: total spend $208M ($86M indirect), current spend under management ~50% targeting 90%, $14.5M unmanaged indirect/tail spend identified, and a conservative model landing at an ~8-month payback with 5-7x 5-year ROI (Jordan Weinberg, Avoma call 18 Jun 2026: 'A conservative P2P business case was presented with an estimated ~8 month payback and 5-7x 5-year ROI'). The buyer actively stress-tested and validated the assumptions on both the 31 Mar 2026 and 18 Jun 2026 calls, including per-category savings math (e.g. '1 percentage point of managed spend ≈ $1,000,000').

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
champion.narrative: Rafal Prylinski (Manager, Indirect Spend) and Jacquelyn Guerrero (Director, Packaging and Indirect Materials) are actively co-leading the internal push: they will 'co-lead the internal presentation to ' and 'support vendor selection discussions post-funding approval' (Avoma notes, 18 Jun 2026). Both are engaged, defending Zycus' conservative approach internally, and have stayed patient through a company restructure and new-CEO transition. Developing, not yet fully proven: they will present funding alone without Zycus support, and their access to/ influence over the Economic Buyer's actual decision is unverified.

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
competition.narrative: A multi-vendor evaluation is confirmed by the buyer ('we've got multiple vendors that we're looking at,' Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026) but no competitor name has been disclosed in any call, task, or field despite being asked. Salesforce's Competitor field is unread/blank. This is a live, material gap heading into vendor selection.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well documented and buyer-confirmed: 75% of Ops/Services and MRO indirect spend (~$14.5M) is unmanaged with minimal category management, MRO has 350+ vendors and only recently moved under Rafal's remit, cycle times run 90-120 days, and AP/PO reconciliation with Oracle is manual. Next Step (dated 1 Jun 2026) also notes 'looking for ways to improve efficiency and limit headcount.' Jacquelyn Guerrero (18 Jun 2026): 'if anything, it further cements the need for p2p for us.'

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are explicitly undefined. Jacquelyn Guerrero stated directly on 18 Jun 2026: 'we're still trying to figure out what we're doing with the contracting, to be honest.' No MSA/SOW discussion, procurement channel, or paper-process owner has surfaced in any call or task to date.

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer role is identified (the Chief Supply Chain Officer, who owns the funding decision) but has never directly engaged with the relevant stakeholder the 18 Jun 2026 call, Rafal Prylinski confirmed 'she is most receptive to her internal team' and the buyer declined an offer to have Zycus in the room for the funding pitch (Steve Ovadje: 'would you like us to be in the room for this presentation'). A VP of Procurement will also review the case in a 'prerun' before the Chief Supply Chain Officer sees it, adding a second unengaged approval layer.

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer laid out the process clearly on 18 Jun 2026: Rafal and Jacquelyn run a prerun with the VP of Procurement, then present the funding case to the Chief Supply Chain Officer in mid-July 2026; only after funding approval does vendor selection begin ('once we have that conversation, then it's really gonna be about... which vendor is right for Amy's,' Jacquelyn Guerrero). This is a multi-step, sequential gate rather than a single approval.

sources: Avoma call 18 Jun 2026 (Business Case Review, Final Draft)
decision_criteria.narrative: Financial criteria are clear (2-3 year ROI target referenced by the buyer, ~90% managed-spend target, savings-rate benchmarks discussed in depth). What is not yet documented is how the Chief Supply Chain Officer or VP of Procurement will formally evaluate/compare vendors once funding is approved - the buyer has said only that vendor selection 'is really gonna be about... which vendor is right for Amy's' after the funding decision (Jacquelyn Guerrero, 18 Jun 2026).

win_position.text: The final ROI/business case (~8-month payback, 5-7x 5-year ROI in the conservative model) was validated line-by-line with the buyer on 18 Jun 2026 and the champions confirmed they remain 'fully on the p2p train.'
The Economic Buyer (Chief Supply Chain Officer) has never engaged with Zycus and declined a seat at the funding pitch, so no one at Zycus has visibility into how the case actually lands.
A confirmed multi-vendor evaluation has an unknown shortlist; Zycus cannot currently size up its real competitive position.

deal_momentum.text: Two-way buyer engagement is intact: an inbound reply from Jacquelyn Guerrero on 4 Jun 2026 re-confirmed active interest, and the 18 Jun 2026 business-case call was substantive and buyer-driven with follow-up questions on integration and inventory.

The deal has sat in Formal Evaluation since 19 Feb 2026 (~140 days) without moving to Shortlisted, driven by legitimate business-case build time and an Amy's internal restructure/new-CEO transition, but stage progression itself has been flat.

customer_commitment.text: Rafal Prylinski and Jacquelyn Guerrero are both named to co-lead the internal funding pitch and to lead vendor-selection discussions post-approval (Avoma notes, 18 Jun 2026).
Commitment is currently champion-level only; the Economic Buyer's own commitment is unverified since she has never met Zycus.

hard.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Neutral' - the buyer's stated AI stance is explicitly Neutral. The Merlin product line is empty, and Products are limited to iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing and Certinal. No Merlin, ANA or Agentic AI is tagged on the opportunity.
• The March 31 2026 ROI/business case call (44 minutes) with Rafal Prylinski, Jacquelyn Guerrero and Rebecah Samo did surface AI-roadmap context. Rafal's action item was to send a survey to Amy's procurement team to gather discovery questions on AI expectations, and the notes record that the initiative is "tied to VP of IT's vision for AI adoption" with the procurement team positioned as the pilot group for AI-driven automation.
• Discussion centered on ROI math, managed spend, tail spend (350+ MRO vendors) and the contract repository - not on Merlin or ANA specifics. Genuine AI exploration is happening (IT-led survey, pilot designation), but the buyer's recorded AI importance is Neutral and AI features are not yet must-haves on this P2P deal. Confidence is medium.
